title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. cespitosa
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. cespitosa, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, characterized by its blue, bell-shaped flowers and compact, cushion-like growth habit. The alpine bellflower has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Alpine bellflower can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of illness.
